text:
Once upon a time in the land of Dreamwood, all the flowers were asleep after a rainstorm. The delicate petals lay curled up in sleep, and none flitted about or sang their sweet songs.

Suddenly, a tiny starwort popped open its eyes. "Peep!" it chirPED.

Soon afterward, a dozen sleepy lavender buds stirred. "Cheep," they murmured.

Then, a solitary dandelion sat on a sunny hillock. It yawned wide. "Blow," it whispered.

The whole field began to buzz with life. The starwort sang louder. The lavender buds hummed. The dandelion laughed.

They all danced together—twirling, jiving, and hopping—in a circle so wide it was almost a blur.

Suddenly, the nightingale from the thicket sang a raucous tune. "Whooo-wee!"
